preamble: The fronting and raising of a back rounded vowel /o/ to a high front vowel /i/, often triggered by a high front sound in the following syllable (umlaut) or as part of a larger vowel shift.

examples.note: Northeastern (Lithuanian/Litvish) Yiddish shifted the diphthong that other dialects realize as /oy/ → /ey/. E.g., Central Yiddish 'toyer' (gate) → Litvish 'teyer'. This o→ei/i vowel shift is a defining Litvish isogloss.
Long /o:/ fronted and raised to /i:/ via i-umlaut.
